message.content:
Search the web for information about Tiller Money AutoCat rules format. I need to understand:
1. What is the AutoCat feature in Tiller Money?
2. What columns/fields are used in AutoCat rules in a spreadsheet?
3. How do the rules match transactions (exact match, contains, regex, etc.)?

Provide a concise summary of the AutoCat rule format and how it works.

content.text: I'll search for information about Tiller Money AutoCat rules format for you.

Tiller Money AutoCat rules matching transactions regex exact match

Links: [{"title":"How to use AutoCat for automatic categorization | Tiller Help Center","url":"https://help.tiller.com/en/articles/3792984-how-to-use-autocat-for-automatic-categorization"},{"title":"AutoCat: Automatic Transaction Categorization In Spreadsheets - Tiller","url":"https://tiller.com/how-tiller-works/autocat/"},{"title":"Using RegEx with AutoCat - Google Sheets - Tiller Community","url":"https://community.tiller.com/t/using-regex-with-autocat/4797"},{"title":"Using AutoCat and Regex - Conversation - Tiller Community","url":"https://community.tiller.com/t/using-autocat-and-regex/14207"},{"title":"What Is Your Favorite AutoCat Regex? - Conversation - Tiller Community","url":"https://community.tiller.com/t/what-is-your-favorite-autocat-regex/14244"},{"title":"Using AI Suggest (Beta) for AutoCat to automatically categorize transactions | Tiller Help Center","url":"https://help.tiller.com/en/articles/9916508-using-ai-suggest-beta-for-autocat-to-automatically-categorize-transactions"},{"title":"AutoCat Categorizing Amazon Transactions - Google Sheets - Tiller Community","url":"https://community.tiller.com/t/autocat-categorizing-amazon-transactions/15117"},{"title":"Order of Autocat Rules - #4 by heather - Ask Anything - Tiller Community","url":"https://community.tiller.com/t/order-of-autocat-rules/24192/4"},{"title":"Using the Tiller Money Feeds add-in for Microsoft Excel | Tiller Help Center","url":"https://help.tiller.com/en/articles/2283741-using-the-tiller-money-feeds-add-in-for-microsoft-excel"},{"title":"Do you really have to create AutoCat rules for every transaction? - Ask Anything - Tiller Community","url":"https://community.tiller.com/t/do-you-really-have-to-create-autocat-rules-for-every-transaction/22244"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about Tiller Money's AutoCat rules and matching transactions:

"Equals" is a keyword for filter criteria that means "text equals entire rule string exactly". This would be the exact match option for AutoCat rules.

AutoCat has the ability to process Regex expressions, making it possible to use regular expressions for more complex pattern matching beyond exact matches. Regex is short for regular expression and is kind of a programming language for pattern matching, making it a great fit for complex AutoCat filters.

For AutoCat rules matching transactions, the system supports various filter criteria keywords beyond "Equals" for exact matching. Many users have built filters using familiar criteria like Contains, while more advanced users have experimented with filters like Starts With, Ends With or Max and Min.

The main Tiller Help Center article on AutoCat discusses these matching options and how to set up categorization rules, though the search results show the "Equals" keyword is specifically for exact text matching.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"How to use AutoCat for automatic categorization | Tiller Help Center","url":"https://help.tiller.com/en/articles/3792984-how-to-use-autocat-for-automatic-categorization"},{"title":"AutoCat: Automatic Transaction Categorization In Spreadsheets - Tiller","url":"https://tiller.com/how-tiller-works/autocat/"},{"title":"Part 3 Of 8: Customizing Your Categories And AutoCat - Tiller","url":"https://tiller.com/courses/getting-started-with-tiller/lessons/getting-started-with-tiller-part-3-of-8-customize-your-categories-and-keep-organized-with-autocat/"},{"title":"How to Run AutoCat on Fill - Helpful Tips - Tiller Community","url":"https://community.tiller.com/t/how-to-run-autocat-on-fill/33487"},{"title":"Using Tiller’s Category Hints | Tiller Help Center","url":"https://help.tiller.com/en/articles/10343918-using-tiller-s-category-hints"},{"title":"AutoCat for Google Sheets | Tiller Help Center","url":"https://help.tillerhq.com/en/articles/3792984-how-to-use-autocat-beta-for-tiller-money-feeds-sheets"},{"title":"Video: How To Automatically Categorize Spreadsheet Transactions With AutoCat - Tiller","url":"https://tiller.com/autocat/"},{"title":"New AutoCat (beta) features in the Tiller Money Feeds add-on for Google Sheets - News & Announcements - Tiller Community","url":"https://community.tiller.com/t/new-autocat-beta-features-in-the-tiller-money-feeds-add-on-for-google-sheets/5106"},{"title":"Tiller Money Review 2022: Next Level Budgeting - One Shot Finance","url":"https://oneshotfinance.com/tiller-review/"},{"title":"Tiller Money Review: Level Up Your Spreadsheet Budget","url":"https://marriagekidsandmoney.com/tiller-money-review/"}]

Based on the search results, here's information about Tiller Money AutoCat rules format:

## AutoCat Rules Format

AutoCat allows you to automatically categorize transactions and override other columns in the Transactions sheet using a customized rule set.

### Basic Rule Structure

Choose a Category from the dropdown, enter "Description Contains" keyword(s) into that column in the ruleset and you have a basic rule.(no content)AutoCat uses filter and override columns in the rule set to understand how to read your Transactions sheet and apply the rules based on your customized criteria.

### Filter Criteria

Filter criteria columns in the rules sheet determine if a transaction should be processed. A column is recognized as a filter criteria when it has a filter criteria suffix (e.g. contains, equals, starts with, ends with).(no content)Filter criteria are AND-ed together— for a transaction to be processed, it must meet all non-blank rules.

### Additional Filter Options

You can also use the Account Contains and Institution Contains in a similar manner to drill down even further. Additionally, you can add tags, bulk-update descriptions, use RegEx, and optimize your data.

### How to Set Up

After install the AutoCat rule sheet (tab) should appear. It comes pre-populated with an example rule.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's how Tiller Money's AutoCat feature works:

## Overview

AutoCat allows you to automatically set the category of a transaction based on the contents of the transaction description.

## How It Works

AutoCat helps you build categorization rules based on the Description column in your Transactions sheet by typing in a keyword into the "When Description contains" field and assigning a category, or selecting one or more rows to help fill in the filter criteria.(no content)A simple "Description Contains" filter works for basic rule sets to capture recurring transactions by choosing a Category from the dropdown and entering keyword(s) into that column in the ruleset.

## Examples

For example, Tiller says that you could have AutoCat file all of your grocery store transactions that are above $10 as "groceries" and under $10 as "Snacks."

## Running AutoCat

After you have built a rule set, you can run AutoCat using the Tiller Money Feeds add-on sidebar by clicking the "Run AutoCat" button in the home sidebar screen.(no content)When you enable the "Auto Run on Fill" feature, Tiller automatically applies your rules to new transactions as they arrive, helping you see where your money is going with less manual effort.

## Key Features

Your rules apply to past transactions as well as new ones that enter your spreadsheet.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: Let me fetch more detailed information from the main Tiller Help Center article to get comprehensive details about the columns and rule format.

input.prompt: What are the column names and fields used in AutoCat rules? What are the filter criteria suffixes? What matching options are available?

content.content:
# AutoCat Rules: Columns, Fields, and Matching Options

## Column Types

AutoCat rules use two types of columns:

1. **Filter Criteria** - Determines if a transaction qualifies for the rule
2. **Override** - Specifies what values to apply to matching transactions

## Filter Criteria Suffixes

Filter criteria columns must end with one of these keywords:

- **Equals** - "text equals entire rule string exactly"
- **Contains** - "text contains rule string"
- **Starts With** - "text starts with rule string"
- **Ends With** - "text ends with rule string"
- **Max** - "value is less than or equal to rule value"
- **Min** - "value is greater than or equal to rule value"
- **Polarity** - Detects positive or negative amounts
- **Regex** - Supports regular expression patterns

## Matching Options

**Multiple Match Criteria**: You can specify multiple values in a single rule. For example: `"Starbucks","Counter Culture","Peets"`. These are OR-ed together, meaning the rule applies if any keyword matches.

**Key Rule**: "Filter criteria are AND-ed together— for a transaction to be processed, it must meet all non-blank rules."

## Important Notes

Column names are case-sensitive. Rules process in top-down order, with specific rules placed above generic ones. Any Transactions sheet column—including custom columns like Tags or Notes—can function as either filter criteria or overrides.

content.text:
## Summary: Tiller Money AutoCat Rules Format

Based on my research, here's a concise overview of the AutoCat feature and how it works:

### 1. What is AutoCat?

**AutoCat** is an automatic transaction categorization feature in Tiller Money that allows you to create rules to automatically categorize transactions and override other columns in your Transactions spreadsheet. Rules can be applied to past transactions and automatically to new ones as they arrive if "Auto Run on Fill" is enabled.

### 2. AutoCat Rule Columns/Fields

AutoCat rules use two types of columns:

- **Filter Criteria Columns** - Determine whether a transaction matches the rule. These must end with a filter suffix (see below)
- **Override Columns** - Specify what values to apply to matching transactions (e.g., Category, Tags, Notes, or custom columns)

The most common override column is **Category** - you select a category from a dropdown for transactions that match the filter criteria.

### 3. Rule Matching Options

AutoCat supports multiple matching criteria through filter suffixes:

| Suffix | Matching Type | Description |
|--------|---------------|-------------|
| **Contains** | Partial match | Text contains the rule string |
| **Equals** | Exact match | Text equals the entire rule string exactly |
| **Starts With** | Prefix match | Text starts with the rule string |
| **Ends With** | Suffix match | Text ends with the rule string |
| **Regex** | Pattern match | Regular expression patterns for complex matching |
| **Min** | Numeric | Value is greater than or equal to rule value |
| **Max** | Numeric | Value is less than or equal to rule value |
| **Polarity** | Amount sign | Detects positive or negative amounts |

### Key Behaviors:

- **Multiple values** in a single rule are **OR-ed together** (e.g., `"Starbucks","Counter Culture","Peets"` matches any of these)
- **Multiple filter criteria columns** are **AND-ed together** (all non-blank rules must match for the transaction to be processed)
- Rules are processed **top-down** in order, with specific rules typically placed above generic ones
- **Column names are case-sensitive**

### Examples:

- Simple rule: "Description Contains" = "Starbucks" → Category = "Coffee"
- Amount rule: "Amount Max" = 10 → Category = "Snacks" (amounts under $10)
- Amount rule: "Amount Min" = 10 → Category = "Groceries" (amounts $10+)
- Regex rule: "Description Regex" = `^(AMZN|Amazon)` → Category = "Shopping"

Sources:
- [How to use AutoCat for automatic categorization | Tiller Help Center](https://help.tiller.com/en/articles/3792984-how-to-use-autocat-for-automatic-categorization)
- [AutoCat: Automatic Transaction Categorization In Spreadsheets - Tiller](https://tiller.com/how-tiller-works/autocat/)
- [Using RegEx with AutoCat - Google Sheets - Tiller Community](https://community.tiller.com/t/using-regex-with-autocat/4797)
